title: CPPI-style soft de-risking glide path for the drawdown control
source: quant-phd-advisor re-review (2026-05-30) under the long-horizon + future-equity frame, verified against a reproduced full-history backtest (scripts/backtest_survival_book.py, 2000-01-03 -> 2026-05-29, 26.4y).
why: Reproduced full-history backtest shows maxDD -10.73%, which EXCEEDS the -10% manual-reset latch (DD breaker latched at end: True; 5092 DD-stops). Over a multi-year production life the binary latch WILL fire and then hold the book flat until a human reset -- a single catastrophic cliff. A CPPI-style glide path scales gross exposure with the cushion above a floor: de-risk continuously as equity approaches the latch, bottoming out exactly at the -10% latch so the two compose rather than fight. Converts the cliff into graded ruin-minimization -- the textbook dynamic-leverage approach for a no-alpha capital-preservation mandate (CPPI floor logic). Quant advisor promoted this to the #1 always-on, equity-agnostic improvement.
value_rationale: H: best ruin-minimization upgrade for a long-lived book; reduces probability of ever hitting the flat-forever latch, the worst outcome for a survival book.
scope: Continuous exposure multiplier m*(equity-floor) on target_vol/k via RiskState; glide bottoms at the existing -10% latch (composes, not fights).

SPECIFY the evaluation point: the fast 60s risk tick (live_loop.py risk_check) only FLATTENS on breach today -- the glide must act there too, else the book sits at full exposure between weekly rebalances during a developing drawdown (the weekend-crypto window the fast monitor exists for). Prove the intraday path.
MEASURE pro-cyclical lock-in as a DISTRIBUTION on B0112 (terminal-equity left tail, time-to-recovery) vs the binary latch -- do not wave it through by assertion.
Keep the manual-reset hard latch as the floor backstop.
Co-validate WITH B0108 on B0112 distributions; isolate the cap's marginal tail first.
recommendation: Hard-gated on B0112. Co-validate with B0108 on bootstrap/stress distributions, never the single path.
out_of_scope: Removing the hard latch; it remains the floor backstop.

history.reason: risk-officer: intraday glide path + measured lock-in + co-validation
from: Continuous exposure multiplier m*(equity - floor) on target_vol / k, threaded through RiskState; glide bottoms at the existing -10% latch level.
Backtest: compare latch-hit frequency, terminal-equity left tail, and time-to-recovery vs the current binary latch.
Accept the pro-cyclical lock-in failure mode (de-risk-then-miss-recovery) as aligned with a survive-first mandate; document the trade-off.
Keep the manual-reset latch as the hard backstop underneath the glide.

Keep the manual-reset hard latch as the floor backstop.
Co-validate WITH B0108 on B0112 distributions; isolate the cap's marginal tail first.
